### Installation

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/SKILL.md

Instructions for installing Streamdown and its optional plugins.

```APIDOC
## Installation

### NPM
```bash
npm install streamdown
```

### Optional Plugins
```bash
npm install @streamdown/code @streamdown/mermaid @streamdown/math @streamdown/cjk
```
```

--------------------------------

### Install @streamdown/math

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/math.mdx

Install the package via npm.

```bash
npm install @streamdown/math
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Install all required project dependencies using pnpm.

```bash
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Install @streamdown/mermaid

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/plugins.md

Install the Mermaid diagram plugin.

```bash
npm install @streamdown/mermaid
```

--------------------------------

### MDX Documentation Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/README.md

Example of an MDX file structure including frontmatter and custom components.

```mdx
---
title: Getting Started
description: Learn how to get started with Geistdocs
---

# Getting Started

Welcome to the documentation!

<Callout type="info">
  This is a custom component you can use in your docs.
</Callout>
```

--------------------------------

### Build and start application

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/README.md

Standard commands for building and starting the Next.js application on supported platforms.

```bash
pnpm build
```

```bash
pnpm start
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vega Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/custom-renderers.mdx

Install the necessary packages for Vega-Lite chart rendering.

```bash
npm install vega vega-lite vega-embed
```

--------------------------------

### Individual Plugin Installation and Usage

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/usage.mdx

Install and configure specific plugins independently based on project requirements.

```bash
npm install @streamdown/code
```

```tsx
import { Streamdown } from 'streamdown';
import { code } from '@streamdown/code';

<Streamdown plugins={{ code: code }}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>
```

```bash
npm install @streamdown/mermaid
```

```tsx
import { Streamdown } from 'streamdown';
import { mermaid } from '@streamdown/mermaid';

<Streamdown plugins={{ mermaid: mermaid }}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>
```

```bash
npm install @streamdown/math
```

```tsx
import { Streamdown } from 'streamdown';
import { math } from '@streamdown/math';
import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css';

<Streamdown plugins={{ math: math }}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>
```

```bash
npm install @streamdown/cjk
```

```tsx
import { Streamdown } from 'streamdown';
import { cjk } from '@streamdown/cjk';

<Streamdown plugins={{ cjk: cjk }}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>
```

--------------------------------

### Install @streamdown/cjk

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Install the package via npm.

```bash
npm install @streamdown/cjk
```

--------------------------------

### Install AntV Infographic

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/custom-renderers.mdx

Install the required package for rendering AntV infographics.

```bash
npm install @antv/infographic
```

--------------------------------

### Run Development Server

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/README.md

Start the local development server to view and test your documentation site.

```bash
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Remend

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/packages/remend/README.md

Install the package via npm.

```bash
npm i remend
```

--------------------------------

### Install Streamdown

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/getting-started.mdx

Use a package manager to install the Streamdown library.

```bash
npm i streamdown
```

--------------------------------

### Theme Examples

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/styling.md

Predefined CSS variable configurations for different visual themes.

```css
:root {
  --primary: 0 0% 20%;
  --muted: 0 0% 96%;
  --border: 0 0% 90%;
  --radius: 0.25rem;
}
```

```css
:root {
  --primary: 217 91% 60%;
  --muted: 214 100% 97%;
  --border: 214 32% 91%;
  --radius: 0.75rem;
}
```

```css
:root {
  --border: transparent;
  --muted: 0 0% 98%;
  --radius: 0rem;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install @streamdown/code

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/code.mdx

Install the package via npm.

```bash
npm install @streamdown/code
```

--------------------------------

### Install Streamdown Plugins

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/usage.mdx

Install necessary packages to enable advanced features like syntax highlighting, diagrams, math, and CJK support.

```bash
npm install @streamdown/code @streamdown/mermaid @streamdown/math @streamdown/cjk
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Static Mode with Plugins and Themes

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/usage.mdx

Advanced configuration example including mermaid diagrams, syntax highlighting themes, and plugin settings.

```tsx
<Streamdown
  mode="static"
  plugins={{
    code: code,
    mermaid: mermaid,
  }}
  shikiTheme={['github-light', 'github-dark']}
  mermaid={{ config: { theme: 'neutral' } }}
>
  {content}
</Streamdown>
```

--------------------------------

### Install AI Elements Message Component

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/getting-started.mdx

Install the message component from the AI Elements library.

```bash
npx ai-elements@latest add message
```

--------------------------------

### Install Streamdown

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/SKILL.md

Install the core Streamdown package using npm.

```bash
npm install streamdown
```

--------------------------------

### Vibrant Blue Theme Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/styling.mdx

Create a vibrant blue theme by adjusting the `--primary`, `--muted`, and `--border` CSS variables. This example also increases the border radius.

```css
:root {
  --primary: 217 91% 60%;
  --muted: 214 100% 97%;
  --border: 214 32% 91%;
  --radius: 0.75rem;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Conventional commit examples

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Examples of commit messages following the conventional commits standard.

```text
feat: add support for custom code block themes
fix: resolve markdown parsing issue with nested lists
docs: update README with new API examples
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Plugin with Factory Function

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/mermaid.mdx

Use createMermaidPlugin for advanced configuration setup.

```tsx
import { createMermaidPlugin } from '@streamdown/mermaid';

const mermaid = createMermaidPlugin({
  config: {
    theme: 'dark',
    fontFamily: 'monospace',
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Minimal Gray Theme Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/styling.mdx

Apply a minimal gray theme by overriding specific CSS variables. This example sets a darker primary color and lighter muted backgrounds.

```css
:root {
  --primary: 0 0% 20%;
  --muted: 0 0% 96%;
  --border: 0 0% 90%;
  --radius: 0.25rem;
}
```

--------------------------------

### AntV Infographic DSL Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/custom-renderers.mdx

Example of the YAML-like DSL used to define an infographic.

```markdown
```infographic
infographic list-row-horizontal-icon-arrow
data
  title Product Development Lifecycle
  desc Complete process from requirements to launch
  items
    - label Research
      value 15
      desc User interviews and competitive analysis
      icon mdi/account-search
    - label Design
      value 42
      desc Interaction prototype and visual design
      icon mdi/palette
    - label Development
      value 65
      desc Implementation and testing
      icon mdi/code-tags
    - label Launch
      value 100
      desc Official release and user feedback
      icon mdi/rocket-launch
```
```

--------------------------------

### CodeHighlighterPlugin Setup

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/api.md

Initializes the code highlighter plugin with specified themes. Use `createCodePlugin` from '@streamdown/code'.

```typescript
import { code } from '@streamdown/code';
import { createCodePlugin } from '@streamdown/code';

const code = createCodePlugin({
  themes: ['github-light', 'github-dark'], // [light, dark]
});

// Methods:
code.highlight(options, callback?);
code.supportsLanguage(language: string): boolean;
code.getSupportedLanguages(): string[];
code.getThemes(): [BundledTheme, BundledTheme];
```

--------------------------------

### MathPlugin Setup

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/api.md

Initializes the Math plugin, optionally enabling single dollar sign text math and setting an error color. Use `createMathPlugin` from '@streamdown/math'.

```typescript
import { math } from '@streamdown/math';
import { createMathPlugin } from '@streamdown/math';

const math = createMathPlugin({
  singleDollarTextMath: true, // default: false
  errorColor: '#ff0000',
});

math.getStyles(); // Returns "katex/dist/katex.min.css"
```

--------------------------------

### Vega-Lite Chart Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/custom-renderers.mdx

Example of a Vega-Lite JSON specification within a code fence, which can be rendered by a custom Vega-Lite renderer.

```markdown
```vega-lite
{
  "$schema": "https://vega.github.io/schema/vega-lite/v5.json",
  "width": "container",
  "height": 200,
  "data": {
    "values": [
      {"month": "Jan", "revenue": 28},
      {"month": "Feb", "revenue": 55},
      {"month": "Mar", "revenue": 43}
    ]
  },
  "mark": "bar",
  "encoding": {
    "x": {"field": "month", "type": "nominal"},
    "y": {"field": "revenue", "type": "quantitative"}
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Mixed Content Formatting

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Example showing seamless integration of CJK and English text.

```markdown
**重要提示（Important Notice）：**请注意。
```

--------------------------------

### DiagramPlugin Setup

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/api.md

Initializes the Mermaid diagram plugin with custom configuration. Use `createMermaidPlugin` from '@streamdown/mermaid'.

```typescript
import { mermaid } from '@streamdown/mermaid';
import { createMermaidPlugin } from '@streamdown/mermaid';

const mermaid = createMermaidPlugin({
  config: {
    theme: 'dark', // 'default' | 'dark' | 'forest' | 'neutral' | 'base'
    fontFamily: 'monospace',
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Rust Code Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/code-blocks.mdx

Example of a Rust code block calculating the sum of a vector of integers.

```rust
fn main() {
    let numbers = vec![1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
    let sum: i32 = numbers.iter().sum();
    println!("Sum: {}", sum);
}
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Flavored Markdown (GFM) Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/index.mdx

Demonstrates tables, task lists, strikethrough, autolinks, and footnotes supported by remark-gfm.

```markdown
| Feature | Supported |
|---------|-----------|
| Tables  | ✓         |
| Tasks   | ✓         |

- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Pending task

~~Strikethrough text~~

```

--------------------------------

### Production Security Configuration

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/security.md

A strict configuration example for handling AI-generated content securely.

```tsx
<Streamdown
  rehypePlugins={[
    rehypeRaw,
    [rehypeSanitize, {}],
    [harden, {
      allowedProtocols: ['https', 'mailto'],
      allowedLinkPrefixes: ['https://your-domain.com'],
      allowedImagePrefixes: ['https://your-cdn.com'],
      allowDataImages: false,
    }],
  ]}
  linkSafety={{
    enabled: true,
    onLinkCheck: async (url) => {
      const trusted = ['your-domain.com'];
      return trusted.some((d) => new URL(url).hostname.endsWith(d));
    },
  }}
>
  {aiContent}
</Streamdown>
```

--------------------------------

### GFM Features Examples

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/features.md

Standard GFM syntax supported by default.

```markdown
| Left | Center | Right |
|:-----|:------:|------:|
| A    | B      | C     |
```

```markdown
- [x] Completed
- [ ] Pending
```

--------------------------------

### CjkPlugin Setup

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/api.md

Initializes the CJK plugin for better handling of East Asian languages. Use `createCjkPlugin` from '@streamdown/cjk'.

```typescript
import { cjk } from '@streamdown/cjk';
import { createCjkPlugin } from '@streamdown/cjk';

const cjk = createCjkPlugin();
// Provides remarkPluginsBefore and remarkPluginsAfter
```

--------------------------------

### Common Mathematical Expressions

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/math.mdx

Examples of common LaTeX syntax for fractions, roots, exponents, Greek letters, summations, integrals, limits, and matrices.

```markdown
$$\frac{numerator}{denominator}$$
```

```markdown
$$\sqrt{x}$$ or $$\sqrt[n]{x}$$
```

```markdown
$$x^2$$ or $$x_i$$ or $$x_i^2$$
```

```markdown
$$\alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta, \theta, \pi, \sigma, \omega$$
$$\Gamma, \Delta, \Theta, \Pi, \Sigma, \Omega$$
```

```markdown
$$\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}$$
```

```markdown
$$\int_{a}^{b} f(x) \, dx$$
```

```markdown
$$\lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{1}{x} = 0$$
```

```markdown
$$
\begin{bmatrix}
a & b \\
c & d
\end{bmatrix}
$$
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced Mathematical Examples

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/math.mdx

Complex equations including the quadratic formula, Euler's identity, normal distribution, Taylor series, and integration by parts.

```markdown
$$
x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}
$$
```

```markdown
$$
e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0
$$
```

```markdown
$$
f(x) = \frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}} e^{-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^2}
$$
```

```markdown
$$
e^x = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{x^n}{n!} = 1 + x + \frac{x^2}{2!} + \frac{x^3}{3!} + \cdots
$$
```

```markdown
$$
\int u \, dv = uv - \int v \, du
$$
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Math Plugin Configuration Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/math.mdx

Configure the math plugin to enable single-dollar text math and set a custom error color. The default for singleDollarTextMath is false.

```tsx
import { Streamdown } from 'streamdown';
import { createMathPlugin } from '@streamdown/math';
import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css';

const math = createMathPlugin({
  singleDollarTextMath: true, // Enable $...$ syntax (default: false)
  errorColor: '#dc2626',      // Custom error color (default: "--color-muted-foreground")
});

export default function Page() {
  return (
    <Streamdown plugins={{ math }}>
      {markdown}
    </Streamdown>
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### No Borders Theme Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/styling.mdx

Achieve a 'no borders' look by setting the `--border` variable to `transparent` and adjusting `--muted` and `--radius` for a cleaner appearance.

```css
:root {
  --border: transparent;
  --muted: 0 0% 98%;
  --radius: 0rem;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Clone the repository

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Initial step to download the project source code to your local machine.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-username/streamdown.git
cd streamdown
```

--------------------------------

### Create Gantt Charts

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/mermaid.mdx

Plan and track project timelines.

```markdown
```mermaid
gantt
    title Project Schedule
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    section Design
    Wireframes       :2024-01-01, 7d
    Mockups         :2024-01-08, 7d
    section Development
    Frontend        :2024-01-15, 14d
    Backend         :2024-01-15, 14d
    section Testing
    QA Testing      :2024-01-29, 7d
```
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshoot Framework Configurations

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/features.md

Configuration snippets for Next.js and Vite to resolve common bundling and styling issues.

```bash
npm install shiki
```

```javascript
// next.config.js
{ transpilePackages: ['shiki'] }
```

```javascript
// vite.config.js
{ ssr: { noExternal: ['streamdown'] } }
```

```javascript
// next.config.js
{
  serverComponentsExternalPackages: ['vscode-jsonrpc'],
  webpack: (config) => {
    config.resolve.alias['vscode-jsonrpc'] = false;
    return config;
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Repository with Template

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/README.md

Use this command to create a new repository from the Geistdocs template.

```bash
gh repo create mydocs --template vercel/geistdocs --clone
cd mydocs
```

--------------------------------

### Create State Diagrams

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/mermaid.mdx

Model state machines and state transitions.

```markdown
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Loading: start
    Loading --> Success: data received
    Loading --> Error: failed
    Success --> Idle: reset
    Error --> Loading: retry
    Success --> [*]
```
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript Code Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/code-blocks.mdx

Example of a TypeScript code block demonstrating an interface and an asynchronous function.

```typescript
interface User {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  email: string;
}

async function fetchUser(id: number): Promise<User> {
  const response = await fetch(`/api/users/${id}`);
  return response.json();
}
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin Quick Reference

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/SKILL.md

A summary of available plugins and their purposes.

```APIDOC
## Plugin Quick Reference

| Plugin | Package | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| Code | `@streamdown/code` | Syntax highlighting (Shiki, 200+ languages) |
| Mermaid | `@streamdown/mermaid` | Diagrams (flowcharts, sequence, etc.) |
| Math | `@streamdown/math` | LaTeX via KaTeX (requires CSS import) |
| CJK | `@streamdown/cjk` | Chinese/Japanese/Korean text support |

**Math requires CSS:**
```tsx
import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css';
```

For plugin configuration details, see [references/plugins.md](references/plugins.md).
```

--------------------------------

### Python Code Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/code-blocks.mdx

Example of a Python code block defining a function to generate Fibonacci sequence.

```python
def fibonacci(n: int) -> list[int]:
    """Generate Fibonacci sequence up to n terms."""
    fib = [0, 1]
    for i in range(2, n):
        fib.append(fib[i-1] + fib[i-2])
    return fib

print(fibonacci(10))
```

--------------------------------

### Create Task Lists

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/gfm.mdx

Define interactive task lists using bracket syntax.

```markdown
- [x] Setup project structure
- [x] Install dependencies
- [ ] Write documentation
- [ ] Deploy to production
```

```markdown
- [ ] Phase 1: Setup
  - [x] Initialize repository
  - [x] Configure build tools
  - [ ] Setup CI/CD
- [ ] Phase 2: Development
  - [ ] Implement features
  - [ ] Write tests
```

```markdown
## Shopping List
- [ ] Milk
- [ ] Eggs
- [x] Bread

> **Note**: Here's a quote with tasks:
> - [x] Complete quote formatting
> - [ ] Add more examples
```

--------------------------------

### Set Custom Start Line for Code Blocks

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/code-blocks.mdx

Specify the starting line number for code blocks using `startLine=N` in the code fence meta. The value must be a positive integer (>= 1).

```typescript
const user = await getUser(id);
const profile = await getProfile(user);
```

--------------------------------

### Create Flowcharts

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/mermaid.mdx

Visualize processes and workflows using flowchart syntax.

```markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Christmas] -->|Get money| B(Go shopping)
    B --> C{Let me think}
    C -->|One| D[Laptop]
    C -->|Two| E[iPhone]
    C -->|Three| F[Car]
```
```

--------------------------------

### Custom Start Line

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/code-blocks.mdx

Set the starting line number for a code block using `startLine=N` in the code fence meta. Line numbers begin at the value you specify instead of 1. The value must be a positive integer (>= 1).

```APIDOC
## Custom start line

Set the starting line number for a code block using `startLine=N` in the code fence meta:

````markdown
```typescript startLine=10
const user = await getUser(id);
const profile = await getProfile(user);
```
````

Line numbers begin at the value you specify instead of 1. The value must be a positive integer (>= 1).
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Streamdown with Plugins

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/plugins.md

Import plugins and pass them to the Streamdown component. Note that KaTeX CSS is required for math support.

```tsx
import { code } from '@streamdown/code';
import { mermaid } from '@streamdown/mermaid';
import { math } from '@streamdown/math';
import { cjk } from '@streamdown/cjk';
import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css'; // Required for math

<Streamdown plugins={{ code, mermaid, math, cjk }}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>
```

--------------------------------

### Build packages

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Compile all packages in the monorepo, which is a prerequisite for running tests.

```bash
pnpm build
```

--------------------------------

### Strikethrough with Punctuation

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Examples of strikethrough formatting correctly with various CJK punctuation marks.

```markdown
~~削除されたテキスト（括弧付き）。~~この文は正しいです。
~~删除的文字（带括号）。~~这个句子是正确的。
~~이 텍스트(괄호 포함)~~를 삭제합니다.
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Next.js for Shiki

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/references/plugins.md

Add shiki to transpilePackages in next.config.js if warnings occur.

```js
transpilePackages: ['shiki'],
```

--------------------------------

### Remend Standalone Usage

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/termination.mdx

Examples of using the Remend library independently to complete markdown strings.

```APIDOC
## Using Remend Standalone

You can use remend independently in your own markdown rendering pipeline:

```typescript
import remend from 'remend';

const partialMarkdown = "This is **incomplete bold";
const completed = remend(partialMarkdown);
// Result: "This is **incomplete bold**"
```

For links, you can use the `linkMode` option to control how incomplete links are handled:

```typescript
import remend from 'remend';

// Default behavior: use placeholder URL
remend("[Click here](http://exampl");
// Result: "[Click here](streamdown:incomplete-link)"

// Text-only mode: display plain text
remend("[Click here](http://exampl", { linkMode: 'text-only' });
// Result: "Click here"
```

See the [remend package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/remend) for more details on standalone usage.
```

--------------------------------

### Remend Custom Handlers

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/termination.mdx

Guide to extending Remend with custom handlers for domain-specific syntax completion.

```APIDOC
## Custom Handlers

You can extend remend with custom handlers to complete your own markers during streaming. This is useful for domain-specific syntax like custom tags or markers that your AI might output.

```typescript
import remend, { type RemendHandler } from 'remend';

const jokeHandler: RemendHandler = {
  name: 'joke',
  handle: (text) => {
    // Complete <<<JOKE>>> marks that aren't closed
    const match = text.match(/<<<JOKE>>>([^<]*)$/);
    if (match && !text.endsWith('<<</JOKE>>>')) {
      return `${text}<<</JOKE>>>`;
    }
    return text;
  },
  priority: 80, // Runs after most built-ins (-10 to 70)
};

const result = remend(content, { handlers: [jokeHandler] });
```

### Handler Interface

Each handler has three properties:

- `name` - Unique identifier for the handler
- `handle` - Transform function that receives text and returns modified text
- `priority` - Optional execution order (lower runs first, default: 100)

### Execution Order

Built-in handlers use priorities 0-75:

| Handler | Priority |
|---------|----------|
| `singleTilde` | 0 |
| `comparisonOperators` | 5 |
| `htmlTags` | 10 |
| `setextHeadings` | 15 |
| `links` | 20 |
| `boldItalic` | 30 |
| `bold` | 35 |
| `italic` | 40-42 |
| `inlineCode` | 50 |
| `strikethrough` | 60 |
| `katex` | 70 |

Custom handlers default to priority 100, running after all built-ins. Set a lower priority to run before specific built-ins.

**Example: Running Before Bold Handling**

To process content before bold formatting is completed (priority 30), set a lower priority:

```typescript
const preprocessHandler: RemendHandler = {
  name: 'preprocess',
  handle: (text) => {
    // Transform content before bold is completed
    return text.replace(/\{\{highlight\}\}/g, '**');
  },
  priority: 25, // Runs before bold (35) and after links (20)
};
```

**Example: Running After All Built-ins**

For post-processing, use the default priority or higher:

```typescript
const postprocessHandler: RemendHandler = {
  name: 'postprocess',
  handle: (text) => {
    // Clean up after all built-in handlers
    return text.trim();
  },
  priority: 150, // Runs after all built-ins
};
```
```

--------------------------------

### Run tests

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Execute the test suite to verify project integrity.

```bash
pnpm test
```

--------------------------------

### Markdown Autolink Boundary Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Demonstrates how the plugin prevents trailing CJK punctuation from being included in an autolink.

```markdown
Check out https://example.com。这是一个链接。
```

--------------------------------

### Create a changeset

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Generate a changeset to track versioning and changelog updates for your changes.

```bash
pnpm changeset
```

--------------------------------

### Italic Text with Punctuation

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Examples of italic text formatting correctly with various CJK punctuation marks.

```markdown
*これは斜体のテキストです（括弧付き）。*この文が後に続いても大丈夫です。
*这是斜体文字（带括号）。*这句子继续也没问题。
*이 텍스트(괄호 포함)*는 기울임꼴입니다.
```

--------------------------------

### Bold Text with Punctuation

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Examples of bold text formatting correctly with various CJK punctuation marks.

```markdown
**日本語の文章（括弧付き）。**この文が後に続いても大丈夫です。
**中文文本（带括号）。**这句子继续也没问题。
**한국어 구문(괄호 포함)**을 강조.
```

--------------------------------

### Tailwind CSS Configuration

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/SKILL.md

Guides for configuring Tailwind CSS to work with Streamdown, including different versions.

```APIDOC
## Tailwind CSS Configuration

### Tailwind v4
Add to `globals.css`:
```css
@source "../node_modules/streamdown/dist/*.js";
```

For specific plugins:
- Code: `@source "../node_modules/@streamdown/code/dist/*.js";`
- CJK: `@source "../node_modules/@streamdown/cjk/dist/*.js";`
- Math: `@source "../node_modules/@streamdown/math/dist/*.js";`
- Mermaid: `@source "../node_modules/@streamdown/mermaid/dist/*.js";`

### Tailwind v3
Add to `tailwind.config.js`:
```js
module.exports = {
  content: [
    "./app/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}",
    "./node_modules/streamdown/dist/*.js",
  ],
};
```
```

--------------------------------

### Run code quality commands

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/README.md

Commands to lint and format the codebase using pnpm.

```bash
pnpm lint
```

```bash
pnpm format
```

--------------------------------

### Demonstrate CJK Formatting Issues

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/cjk.mdx

Example of CommonMark/GFM limitation where emphasis markers fail near ideographic punctuation.

```markdown
**この文は太字になりません（This won't be bolded）。**この文のせいで（It is due to this sentence）。
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Element Filtering in Streamdown

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/configuration.mdx

Examples of using allowedElements, disallowedElements, and allowElement to control which Markdown elements are rendered.

```tsx
// Only allow paragraphs, links, and emphasis
<Streamdown allowedElements={["p", "a", "em"]}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>

// Remove images but keep everything else
<Streamdown disallowedElements={["img"]}>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>

// Remove images but keep their alt text
<Streamdown disallowedElements={["img"]} unwrapDisallowed>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>

// Custom filter: remove all h3+ headings
<Streamdown
  allowElement={(element) =>
    !["h3", "h4", "h5", "h6"].includes(element.tagName)
  }
>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>
```

--------------------------------

### Customizing Streamdown Plugins

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/index.mdx

Example of how to customize remark and rehype plugins by providing custom arrays, including default plugins.

```tsx
import { Streamdown, defaultRemarkPlugins, defaultRehypePlugins } from 'streamdown';

// Use all defaults
<Streamdown>{markdown}</Streamdown>

// Customize plugins
<Streamdown
  remarkPlugins={[...Object.values(defaultRemarkPlugins), myCustomPlugin]}
  rehypePlugins={[...Object.values(defaultRehypePlugins), anotherPlugin]}
>
  {markdown}
</Streamdown>

```

--------------------------------

### Default rehype-harden Configuration

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/plugins/index.mdx

Illustrates the default security settings for links and images when using rehype-harden.

```json
{
  allowedImagePrefixes: ['*'],
  allowedLinkPrefixes: ['*'],
  allowedProtocols: ['*'],
  defaultOrigin: undefined,
  allowDataImages: true,
}

```

--------------------------------

### Get Random Quote

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/packages/streamdown/__tests__/__fixtures__/code-block-big-html.html

Selects and returns a random quote from a predefined list of quotes. The 'quotes' array must be initialized.

```javascript
getRandomQuote() {
  const randomIndex = Math.floor(Math.random() * this.quotes.length);
  return this.quotes[randomIndex];
}
```

--------------------------------

### Programmatic Code Highlighting

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/code-blocks.mdx

Demonstrates how to programmatically check language support and highlight code using the `code` plugin.

```tsx
import { code } from '@streamdown/code';

// Check language support
if (code.supportsLanguage('typescript')) {
  code.highlight(
    { code: 'const x = 1;', language: 'typescript', themes: ['github-light', 'github-dark'] },
    (result) => {
      // Handle highlighted tokens
      console.log(result.tokens);
    }
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### Usage with AI Streaming (Vercel AI SDK)

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/skills/streamdown/SKILL.md

Example of integrating Streamdown with the Vercel AI SDK for real-time chat responses.

```APIDOC
## Usage with AI Streaming (Vercel AI SDK)

```tsx
'use client';
import { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react';
import { Streamdown } from 'streamdown';
import { code } from '@streamdown/code';

export default function Chat() {
  const { messages, input, handleInputChange, handleSubmit, isLoading } = useChat();

  return (
    <>
      {messages.map((msg, i) => (
        <Streamdown
          key={msg.id}
          plugins={{ code }}
          caret="block"
          isAnimating={isLoading && i === messages.length - 1 && msg.role === 'assistant'}
        >
          {msg.content}
        </Streamdown>
      ))}
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input value={input} onChange={handleInputChange} disabled={isLoading} />
      </form>
    </>
  );
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Markdown Example for Link Restriction

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/security.mdx

Illustrates how safe and unsafe links are handled with domain restrictions. Unsafe links are rendered as '[blocked]'.

```markdown
[Safe link](https://github.com/vercel/streamdown)
[Unsafe link](https://malicious-site.com)
```

--------------------------------

### Development Commands

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/README.md

Common commands for developing and deploying the Geistdocs project.

```bash
# Start development server
pnpm dev

# Build for production
pnpm build

# Start production server
pnpm start
```

--------------------------------

### Restrict Link Domains with rehype-harden

Source: https://github.com/vercel/streamdown/blob/main/apps/website/content/docs/security.mdx

Example of restricting allowed link domains in Streamdown. Links not matching `allowedLinkPrefixes` are rewritten to `defaultOrigin`.

```tsx
import { Streamdown, defaultRehypePlugins } from 'streamdown';
import { harden } from 'rehype-harden';

export default function Page() {
  return (
    <Streamdown
      rehypePlugins={[
        defaultRehypePlugins.raw,
        defaultRehypePlugins.sanitize,
        [
          harden,
          {
            defaultOrigin: 'https://streamdown.ai',
            allowedLinkPrefixes: [
              'https://streamdown.ai',
              'https://github.com',
              'https://vercel.com',
            ],
          },
        ],
      ]}
    >
      {markdown}
    </Streamdown>
  );
}
```
